description: HP 3335A Synthesizer/Level Generator (200 Hz to 80.999999999 MHz). HP-IB remote programming uses single-character function codes followed by digits and a single-character unit suffix, with no spaces (e.g. 'F12.34M' = 12.34 MHz, 'A40.01M' = -40.01 dBm, 'FI4K' = freq increment 4 kHz). Unit suffix codes are context dependent: M = MHz or -dBm, K = kHz or +dBm, H = Hz or degrees. The 3335A is a listen-only device; no talk/query or status readback is documented. Programming codes from Table 3-5 of the Operating Manual; special-attenuator codes from Table 3-6.

commands.name: frequency
commands.description: Selects the frequency function and sets the output frequency. Enter the value then a unit suffix (M=MHz, K=kHz, H=Hz). Code 'F' = ASCII 'F' (decimal 70).

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.range: 200 Hz to 80.999999999 MHz (75 ohm); unit M=MHz, K=kHz, H=Hz

commands.name: amplitude
commands.description: Selects the amplitude function and sets the output level. Enter the value then a level unit suffix (K=+dBm, M=-dBm). Code 'A' = ASCII 'A' (decimal 65).

parameters.name: amplitude
parameters.range: +11.25 dBm to -88.74 dBm (75 ohm); unit K=+dBm, M=-dBm

commands.name: frequency_increment
commands.description: Programs the frequency increment (step size) used by INCR up/down. The 'I' instruction acts on the currently selected function; precede with 'F' to ensure a frequency increment (e.g. 'FI4K'). Code 'I' = ASCII 'I' (decimal 73).

parameters.name: increment
parameters.range: frequency step; unit M=MHz, K=kHz, H=Hz

commands.name: amplitude_increment
commands.description: Programs the amplitude increment (step size) used by INCR up/down. The 'I' instruction acts on the currently selected function; precede with 'A' to ensure an amplitude increment (e.g. 'AI2K'). Code 'I' = ASCII 'I' (decimal 73).

parameters.name: increment
parameters.range: amplitude step in dB; unit K=+dBm, M=-dBm

commands.name: phase_increment
commands.description: Programs the phase increment in degrees (entered with the H = degrees unit suffix). Stepping with INCR up/down then advances the output phase relative to the reference. Code 'P' = ASCII 'P' (decimal 80). May also be reached via 'PI'.

commands.name: increment_up
commands.description: Steps the active parameter (frequency, amplitude or phase) up by one programmed increment. Code 'U' = ASCII 'U' (decimal 85).

commands.name: increment_down
commands.description: Steps the active parameter (frequency, amplitude or phase) down by one programmed increment. Code 'D' = ASCII 'D' (decimal 68).

commands.name: sweep_width
commands.description: Selects the sweep width (span) function and sets the sweep width, centered on the current frequency. Enter the value then a frequency unit suffix (M=MHz, K=kHz, H=Hz). Code 'W' = ASCII 'W' (decimal 87).

parameters.name: sweep_width
parameters.range: sweep span; unit M=MHz, K=kHz, H=Hz

commands.name: goto_start_frequency
commands.description: Goes to (displays and outputs) the sweep start frequency, half the sweep width below the center frequency. Code 'G' = ASCII 'G' (decimal 71).

commands.name: start_single_sweep_10s
commands.description: Initiates a single sweep of approximately 10 seconds (about 100 steps), covering the sweep width centered on the current frequency. Code 'X' = ASCII 'X' (decimal 88).

commands.name: start_single_sweep_50s
commands.description: Initiates a single sweep of approximately 50 seconds (about 1000 steps). Code 'Y' = ASCII 'Y' (decimal 89).

commands.name: start_auto_sweep
commands.description: Initiates a continuous (auto) sweep, repeatedly sweeping from the start frequency until stopped. Code 'Z' = ASCII 'Z' (decimal 90).

commands.name: stop_sweep
commands.description: Stops sweep mode operation and returns to fixed (non-sweep) output. Code 'Q' = ASCII 'Q' (decimal 81).

commands.name: store
commands.description: Stores the current operating parameters (frequency, amplitude, freq incr, amptd incr, phase incr, sweep width) into a preset register. Follow 'S' with the register number 0 through 7. Code 'S' = ASCII 'S' (decimal 83).

commands.name: recall
commands.description: Recalls operating parameters from a preset register. Follow 'R' with the register number 0 through 7. Code 'R' = ASCII 'R' (decimal 82).

commands.name: display_last_entry
commands.description: Displays the last data entry. Code 'L' = ASCII 'L' (decimal 76).

commands.name: back_space
commands.description: Back space: deletes the last entered digit during data entry. Code 'B' = ASCII 'B' (decimal 66).

commands.name: clear
commands.description: Clears the current data entry. Code 'C' = ASCII 'C' (decimal 67).

commands.name: unit_mhz_minus_dbm
commands.description: Unit/terminator suffix. Terminates a frequency entry as MHz, or an amplitude entry as -dBm. ASCII 'M' (decimal 77).

commands.name: unit_khz_plus_dbm
commands.description: Unit/terminator suffix. Terminates a frequency entry as kHz, or an amplitude entry as +dBm. ASCII 'K' (decimal 75).

commands.name: unit_hz_deg
commands.description: Unit/terminator suffix. Terminates a frequency entry as Hz, or a phase entry as degrees. ASCII 'H' (decimal 72).

commands.name: negative_sign
commands.description: Minus sign used within a numeric data entry (e.g. negative amplitude values). ASCII '-' (decimal 45).
examples: A-23.45 (followed by a level unit suffix)

commands.name: special_attenuator_normal
commands.description: Special attenuator program codes (Table 3-6). 'T' = ASCII 'T' (decimal 84) selects straight-through (all sections through). Codes T and 0 through 9 directly control the attenuator sections (AT0-AT7); 'C' returns the instrument to normal amplitude operation. See manual Table 3-6 for the section-by-section bit pattern of each code.

parameters.name: attenuator_code
parameters.range: T (straight through), 0-9 (direct attenuator control), C (return to normal amplitude operation)
